---
title: "V — Signals, control, power, and communication"
description: "Cross-domain models for information, inference, feedback, energy, motion, and links."
page-role: part
part-id: domains
---

This part develops domain models that sit between component behavior and a
complete system. Each model deliberately discards detail; engineering judgment
lies in knowing what it preserves, where it fails, and how to test the boundary.

## Part question

How do we represent, sample, transform, control, power, move, and communicate
with physical signals while preserving the quantities that matter?

## Entry knowledge

Frequency response, dynamic circuits, measurement uncertainty, and basic analog
and embedded implementation are assumed. Use [M02](../appendices/m02-calculus-linear-differential.qmd)
for continuous and discrete dynamics and [M03](../appendices/m03-probability-numerical.qmd)
for noise, estimation, and numerical decisions.

## Learning arc

| Chapters | Development |
|---|---|
| S01–S03 | represent signals and systems, cross the analog/digital boundary, and process sampled data |
| S04 | close a feedback loop and relate performance to stability and robustness |
| S05–S06 | convert, store, and control electrical energy and motion |
| S07 | encode information onto physical communication channels and antennas |
| S08 | fit learned inference to a microcontroller within latency, memory, and energy budgets |
| S09 | run a system from solar, storage, and ambient energy within a truthful energy budget |
| S10 | move small messages over long-range, low-power links within regulatory and duty-cycle limits |

## Exit capability

You should be able to choose a useful time-, frequency-, energy-, or information-
domain model; allocate a sampling, stability, power, or link budget; state the
model’s neglected effects; and design a measurement that tests the decision
made from it.

Labs L13, L14, L16, and L17 anchor the control, power, mixed-signal, and radio
evidence. These domains converge in the projects and complete-system studies
that follow; see the
[practice and project spine](../roadmap.qmd#practice-and-project-spine).
